Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family, COMT subfamily
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. Cation-independent O-methyltransferase family. COMT subfamily. {ECO:0000255|PROSITE-ProRule:PRU01020}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=6-demethylmitomycin A + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = mitomycin A + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:45104, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:84589, ChEBI:CHEBI:84955; EC=2.1.1.316; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:17461583}; CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=6-demethylmitomycin B + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = mitomycin B + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:45100, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:84590, ChEBI:CHEBI:84952; EC=2.1.1.316; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:17461583};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Involved in the biosynthesis of the quinone methoxy group present in the mitomycin A and B, which are used as anticancer agents (PubMed:10099135, PubMed:17461583). In vitro, catalyzes the 6-O-methylation of both C9-beta- and C9-alpha-configured 6-hydroxymitomycins via the transfer of the S-methyl group of S-adenosyl-L-methionine (AdoMet) to the 6-demethylmitomycin A and B. It can also use hydroxyquinone as substrate (PubMed:17461583). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:10099135, ECO:0000269|PubMed:17461583}.
